sn.s: Am Wucheänd gani mit em Lukas i d'Bärg.
sn.e: On the weekend I go to the mountains with Lukas.

sn.s: Mir nämed d'Bahn uf de Pilatus.
sn.e: We take the cable car up the Pilatus.

sn.s: "Vo obe gseht mer ganz Luzärn," seit er.
sn.e: "From up top you can see all of Lucerne," he says.

sn.s: Mir wandered es Stück uf em Grat.
sn.e: We hike a stretch along the ridge.

sn.s: D'Luft isch frisch und chli chalt.
sn.e: The air is fresh and a little cold.

sn.s: "Es bitzli, aber es lohnt sich," säg ich.
sn.e: "A little, but it's worth it," I say.

sn.s: Am Gipfel mached mir e Pause.
sn.e: At the summit we take a break.

sn.s: Unter öis ligt de See, blau und ruhig.
sn.e: Below us lies the lake, blue and calm.

sn.s: "Das isch mini Heimat," seit de Lukas stolz.
sn.e: "This is my home," Lukas says proudly.

sn.s: D'Natur z'Luzärn nimmt eim de Atem.
sn.e: Nature in Lucerne takes your breath away.

sn.s: Müed aber glücklich gani am Aabig hei.
sn.e: Tired but happy, I go home in the evening.
